title: To The Moon.
content:
1.
    Art thou pale for weariness
    Of climbing heaven and gazing on the earth,
    Wandering companionless
    Among the stars that have a different birth, -
    And ever changing, like a joyless eye
    That finds no object worth its constancy?

    2.
    Thou chosen sister of the Spirit,
    That grazes on thee till in thee it pities...
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "To The Moon.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
